Let's have him talk about drugs." I was like, "Well, in order to not further perpetuate that stereotype, I also want to talk about how to make like millions and millions of dollars per year with your personal brand." How's that sound?   [00:08]  Yeah.   [00:09]  Yeah. All right. So, um, some of y'all make more money than me, right? It's cool. But there'll be something for you guys to take away. We did like, uh, 6.5 million last year.   [00:20]  And, uh, but I've been doing this for a long time. I've been in this game for like 10 years. So, a lot of stuff I'm going to tell you, it's going to be principles for you to sustain it for a long time. I'm 42. So, I've been doing like 10 years straight making like millions where a lot of you guys are newer than that, you know. I kind of just want to get to the meat of the presentation cuz I mean, we all have like similar stories. You guys didn't grow up rich, did you? Yeah. So, we all got similar stories. Grew up in the hood, started moving dope, got in some shootouts, like same old story.   [01:00]  You guys are all the same. So, I'm going skip all that and go straight to um how to make a bunch of money online. All right. So, for me, there's like four pillars. [01:35]  They'll be fucking personal trainers or people who want to just get into that lifestyle. Maybe they're not trainers, whatever. We just teach them how to start training people online in exchange for currency. All right.   [01:48]  So, the four pillars of this are first, it's content. I'm doing the best I can with this fucking board. All right. Oh, also, all right, that says content. It's not me, it's the board. However, my handwriting is bad and I don't spell words very well. I was diagnosed with dyslexia as a child. So, if you make fun of me, it's a hate crime.   [02:10]  All right? You're going to it's going it's punishable. God's going to punish you for that. But it all started, we made millions with just organic content before we started running ads.   [02:50]  All right, so first I want to talk about content. I know my man said three times a week, but I think here's what I think. Okay, so I read this book like years ago, maybe it was over 10 years ago and it was called The Long Tail by a dude named Chris Anderson.   [03:50]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "The Long Tail Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  10+ anos atrás   VISUAL:  Conceito de cauda longa vs. grandes sucessos iniciais, gráfico comparativo de estratégias de conteúdo   [03:55]  You don't have to read it. I'm going tell you the most important part. And basically he said that you know like big blockbuster films or maybe like you know A-list artists they try to make as much money as possible in the beginning. That's why when the album comes out first week sales or the first week at the box office and then it kind of just dies over a long period of time.   [04:15]  But in his book, The Long Tale, he was talking about how the internet kind of changed things where instead of trying to have like a big smash hit, you can just have a ton of content and over a long period of time, you still get you'll still kind of get that spike initially, but it won't be as long. But if you have tons of content over a long period of time, you can actually do better than this. Does that make sense?   [04:40]  I hope so. I don't know how well I articulated that. So, after I read that book, I was like, "Yeah, fuck this shit. I'm not going to try to go viral or try to have a video that every make every video a million fucking views. I'm just going to pump out as much content as possible. So, what I started doing initially, I was doing a video every day."   [05:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Daily Video Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  1 vídeo por dia, 12 anos atrás   VISUAL:  Estratégia de volume sobre perfeição, produção diária consistente   [05:05]  And this one I had jobs and shit you know, at the time, this was 12 years ago. I was like, I'm going to put out a YouTube video every day. And it was a lot more difficult back then cuz you know the iPhone that just came out it didn't even do HD like I'm old you know.   [05:25]  And um if I and I had to buy a fucking cheap camera off of eBay and I had to steal a copy of Sony Vegas and build a computer cuz I didn't have money for a laptop. Like laptops are cheaper now but I had to learn how to build a computer, steal a copy of the software and then teach myself how to edit.   [05:45]  But I was like fuck it. I'm going to do it every day while working like I had like three four jobs at the time. It was it was intense. But anyway, I just pumped out as much content as possible. So, I know a lot of y'all might be paralyzed with content cuz what I see with people, they want to make that shit perfect.   [06:05]  They want that best fucking man. I just want to put out quality over quantity. But that shit is it's a misnomer because quality I don't have to draw it. Quality comes from quantity, right? How do you get good at something? You do it a bunch of times, right?   [06:20]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Quality Through Quantity"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Conceito de melhoria através de repetição, curva de aprendizado ascendente   [06:25]  So I was like, "Fuck it. I'm just going to put out a video every day and I'm going to get good at making a good video every day, but I'm just going to put one out every day." And the reason that helps because the more you do it, the better you get at it.   [06:40]  So the first one sucked, but it's cool because like people ain't really watching, so who gives a fuck right? But what I would see some other people who I knew, they would like agonize over one video trying to make it perfect and then if it doesn't blow up, they're like all disappointment. I'm like, "Fuck it. I got another one coming tomorrow."   [07:00]  So I'm not I wasn't attached to the outcome of the content. Does that make sense? So even now I don't give a I don't even look at individual videos or individual pieces of content on any social media to see how well it did. I just look at the overall stats.   [07:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Detachment from Individual Results"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Foco em estatísticas agregadas vs. performance individual, visão macro do desempenho   [07:20]  Does that make sense? I don't know how much views my last video got. I can but I look at the fucking monthly weekly statistics. Are we is it uptrend or a downtrend? And that keeps me unemotional about that one piece of content cuz fuck that shit. You feel me?   [07:40]  So it's like really volume is the only thing that's going to make you better. I don't care how much you fucking read, how much you study. You can read a course, you can take a course about swimming, read a book about it, but until you get in the fucking water, you ain't gonna be you ain't going to be good at it. Does that make sense? [09:55]  So, we do it at the crib and I have like 20 shirts laid out and I just keep changing shirts and then I have these like LED lights behind me to like change the color of the wall and I'll keep changing the colors to match the shirts and shit so it doesn't look like I filmed all that shit in 8 hours even though, you know. So, when I my whole formula, this is how I write. I mean, I've written copy that's made like millions. Like I've written copy for other people, sales pages, emails for myself.   [21:40]  And my formula is the same one every time, right? So, I call it the epic formula. I don't remember if I invented it or stole it, right? But it's epic, right?   [21:55]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "EPIC Formula"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Framework de copywriting em 4 partes escritas no quadro   [22:00]  So, the E stands for engagement. So, I got to get their attention somehow. That's the title, the thumbnail, and the first fucking I say, right? And it's the same with the same with my ads. It's the same with any form of copy. I wrote this formula.   [22:20]  So, I got to get their attention somehow, right? The P is that I kind of tell them what I'm going to tell them. It stands for promise. So, this stands for engagement. This stands for promise. I don't have confidence in my ability to spell all those words, right? But you guys can take your own notes, right?   [22:45]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "EPIC Breakdown"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  E = Engagement | P = Promise | I = Info | C = Call to Action   [22:50]  So, promise, I tell them what I'm going to tell them, right? Why they should pay attention, why it's important, kind of like your why in your system, right? And then the I is where I give them the information. This is like the meat of it. I give them the info. That's what I stands for, info.   [23:15]  And then C, call to action. You know, do something, you know, go to this download this thing or sign up for this webinar or whatever. And that's how I do all my copy, man. I've read a bunch of books on copywriting and I always just come back to that. I might throw other shit in there, right? But that's the format of it.   [23:40]  And it's the same. I can do this for a whole I've done like workshops for the whole day and it's be like based off this or I've done fucking webinars, one minute YouTube, one minute shorts, fucking one hour videos. This is the whole formula every time.   [24:00]  [Pergunta sobre E ser visual ou verbal]   [24:05]  It's well it depends, right? I know that was all that that was only going to young men.   [24:50]  So, it's like, man, put them chicks on the trampoline, you feel me? And that shit did well. [25:10]  Zany, you don't want to you can go viral being zany or silly and shit then you out here hustling. You out here hustling zany, man. You don't want to be like that. I don't want to get I don't want to get coach. I'm not gonna pay Flavor Flav $10,000 for coaching, you know.   [25:30]  So, you got to keep that your reputation in mind as well. And sometimes I cross that line a little bit. I know how much I can cross that line. But you know, but I don't do no You can't be out here doing no clown shit.   [26:00]  You know, even if it's a trend and you see people jumping on this trend doing clown shit. It's like if a king if a king joins a circus, circus doesn't look like a palace. The the king looks like a clown. You know what I'm saying? Y'all should write that down. All right. Um, so don't join the fucking circus unless that's your fucking right? That's your brand. You the zany fucking. You can make money doing zany shit but it's hard to it's hard to sell high ticket coaching doing wacky shit you know?   [26:50]  All right. Then from there, it's the funnels. So, man, y'all y'all don't know how lucky y'all are y'all started. If y'all started in the last 5 years, cuz when I started, I had to use HTML to make my first funnel.   [27:10]  Like I straight up had to I remember I went to six pack shortcuts landing page and I hit that tab where you can see the HTML and I stole that shit and then I put it in some fucking website. It was called Dreamweaver. Microsoft Dreamweaver old school shit.   [27:30]  And I put their HTML in and I changed shit I had the code. Now we got Clickfunnels and Shopify. Y'all lucky. But anyway, the funnels. So I always have I have a CTA at the end of everything. Epic formula stands for CTA and you always got to have CTA.   [27:55]  So I'm maybe not the short form content sometimes because it's difficult to do it like YouTube shorts doesn't really have it but anyway I bring them to a funnel. So I have two or three main funnels.   [28:15]  So one funnel is a book funnel. This book has made what? This funnel has made well over $10 million.   [28:25]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Book Funnel Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  $10M+ em receita gerada   VISUAL:  Funil de livro como lead magnet principal, estratégia de múltiplos pontos de conversão   [28:30]  All right, let me show you what it looks like. It's the landing page is like, yo, it's a video. No, it's not even a video. It's just, yo, boom, get you this book, fool. And they put your contact in here.   [28:50]  Then you get then it's the book, right? Then the thank you page. This is everybody. Thank you page. I don't give them the book on the thank you page. What I do is we do high ticket sales. So, it's like sales team, all that shit right?   [29:10]  It's a video of me saying, "Yo, man, congratulations on downloading the book." Blah blah blah blah blah. It's in the email right now. [95:20]  There's a lot of studies that show it's a nootropic. Nicotine. Start fucking with the nicotine man. I'm telling. I heard Hormozi even said this. Tons tons of energy. It makes you not hungry. It really worked. This guy knows what I'm talking about.   [98:55]  And it's like that. So it's kind of like, you know, there's two ways to make a car go forward. Let's go.     📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO   NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS: Receita Anual:  $6.5M em 2023 Anos de Experiência:  10 anos no negócio online, 42 anos de idade Seguidores Totais:  YouTube: 1.2M subscribers, 500M+ views | Instagram: 1M+ | Facebook: 1.5M | TikTok: 500K Book Funnel Performance:  $10-15M+ em receita total Close Rate:  47% no último mês (já chegou a 50%+) Preço da Oferta:  $10K (mínimo $1K/mês em planos de pagamento) Ticket Médio de Pagamento:  ~$3K Equipe Total:  40 funcionários Sales Team:  5 setters (mulheres) + 3 closers (homens) Produção de Conteúdo:  1 long-form/dia + 2-3 short-form/dia Bulk Filming:  50 vídeos curtos em 2 dias, 2x por mês Vídeos por Live:  4-5 vídeos extraídos de cada YouTube Live (2x/semana) Loom Videos:  7-20 por dia (2 minutos cada) Emails:  1 por dia para toda lista Business Assessment:  60 minutos de vídeo obrigatório + quiz Testemunhos no Livro:  10 vídeos (5-10 min cada) Testemunhos no Assessment:  20 vídeos Saúde:  8 anos sem ficar doente Suplementação:  TRT (3 anos), Cafeína (1,000mg/dia), Nicotina (patches), Bronkaid + 300mg cafeína, Modafinil, Adderall (uso estratégico)   ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:   Conteúdo: Long Tail Strategy:  Volume massivo > viralidade individual Produção em Lote:  Múltiplas roupas + LED lights para simular dias diferentes YouTube Live Hack:  Lives 2x/semana = fonte de múltiplos vídeos editados EPIC Formula:  Engagement → Promise → Info → Call to Action (usado em tudo) Retargeting:  50% view time = melhores adsets ChatGPT para Ideias:  Análise de 50 top videos → novas ideias Desapego de Resultados:  Foco em estatísticas agregadas, não vídeos individuais   Funis: Book Funnel:  Landing page → Thank you page (aplicação) → Email (landing page + aplicação) → Livro PDF (10 capítulos, cada um com vídeo testemunho + aplicação) Múltiplos Pontos de Conversão:  2 oportunidades antes do livro + 10 no livro = 12 chances de aplicar Email Diário:  EPIC formula, principal fonte de vendas recorrentes   Vendas: Two-Call Close:  10 min (setters femininas) + 60 min (closers) Lok Rule:  $1K investimento = 1 hora de conteúdo consumido 60-Min Mandatory Training:  Vídeo + quiz estruturado para baixa pontuação (média 40%) Personal Loom Videos:  Brandon grava 2 min para cada prospect no dia anterior Pre-Sell Massivo:  20 testemunhos no training + baixa pontuação = necessidade clara Objetivo:  Transformar vendedores em "order takers"   Follow-up: Notion Board:  Tracking de todos leads, última data de contato Contato Semanal:  Mínimo 1x/semana com cada lead Content Sharing:  Testemunhos de resultados dos alunos Long-term:  Alguns fecham após 1-2 anos   Gestão de Sales Team: Life Tracking:  Sono, macros, workout, humor em dashboard Love Languages:  Tracking dos cônjuges da equipe Life Coaching:  Intervenção em problemas pessoais afetando performance PDF Framework:  Projects/Conflicts → Plan/Delegate/Fuck it Planning Sessions:  Agendar tempo para planejar, não executar   ESTRUTURA:   Produção de Conteúdo: 2 editores (1 NY, 1 Londres) para aproveitar fusos ISO Mini switcher para 5 câmeras simultâneas Agendamento automático via Meta Business Suite e YouTube VidIQ para ideação   Funil de Conversão: Conteúdo Orgânico → Landing Page Thank You Page (vídeo + aplicação) Email → Landing Page 2 (vídeo + aplicação + link livro) Livro PDF (10 capítulos × EPIC formula × testemunho × aplicação) Setter Call (10 min, feminina, video intro) Personal Loom do Brandon 60-min Training + Quiz obrigatório Closer Call (60 min) Follow-up semanal via Notion   Automação: Zapier: Go High Level → Spreadsheet → Slack → Closers Zapier: Calls agendadas → Spreadsheet para Looms do Brandon Go High Level para agendamentos e SMS Notion para CRM e follow-up tracking   DADOS FINANCEIROS: Revenue 2023:  $6.5M Book Funnel Lifetime:  $10-15M+ Oferta Principal:  $10K Payment Plans:  Até $1K/mês (média ~$3K coletado) Close Rate:  47% (já chegou a 50%+) Chargeback Management:  Sem collections por ser personal brand Whop:  Nova plataforma de funding para melhorar payment plans   DURAÇÃO:  ~102 minutos  FORMATO:  Apresentação presencial em evento, demonstração ao vivo com quadro branco, Q&A interativo
